full_text_rationale: Full text describes OpenGame's evolving Template Skill and Debug Skill, which accumulate reusable project skeletons and verified debugging protocols for web-game coding, with OpenGame-Bench evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 011; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Yilei Jiang, Jinyuan Hu, Qianyin Xiao, Yaozhi Zheng, Ruize Ma, Kaituo Feng, Jiaming Han, Tianshuo Peng, Kaixuan Fan, Manyuan Zhang, Xiangyu Yue
titulo: OpenGame: Open Agentic Coding for Games
formato_armazenamento: Game Skill with Template Skill and Debug Skill. Template Skill stores an evolving library of reusable project skeleton families; Debug Skill stores a living debugging protocol of error signatures, root causes, verified fixes, and pre-execution validations.
framework_agente: OpenGame web-game coding framework with Game Skill, Template Skill, Debug Skill, GameCoder-27B, and OpenGame-Bench
llm_utilizado: GameCoder-27B, Qwen-3.5-27B, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.1, Gemini 3.1 Pro, DeepSeek V3.2, Kimi K2.5, MiniMax m2.5, GLM-4.5
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; dynamic browser execution; VLM judging; model comparison; agent workflow ablation; game-skill maturity ablation; bounded debugging analysis; genre breakdown
benchmarks_utilizados: OpenGame-Bench with 150 natural-language web-game prompts across platformers, top-down shooters, puzzle games, arcade classics, and strategy
metricas_desempenho: Build Health, Visual Usability, Intent Alignment, valid-run means over three seeds, debugging-iteration sensitivity, genre-level Intent Alignment
metricas_cl: Template Skill maturity from meta-template to five template families; Debug Skill maturity from static checklist to post-execution fixes and full living protocol; bounded iterative debugging.
baseline_comparado: Direct LLMs, qwen-code, Cursor, Qwen-3.5-27B, GameCoder-27B, ablations without hook-driven implementation, three-layer reading, physics-first classification, evolved templates, and living debug protocol
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Template Skill evolves from one game-agnostic meta-template to five specialized families; Debug Skill grows with structured verified fixes and reusable inconsistency rules.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; the paper reports bounded automated debugging iterations but not token or monetary cost.
resultado_principal: OpenGame with Claude Sonnet 4.6 achieves Build Health 72.4, Visual Usability 67.2, and Intent Alignment 65.1 on OpenGame-Bench.
desempenho_vs_baseline: OpenGame with Claude Sonnet 4.6 outperforms Cursor with Claude Sonnet 4.6 by 5.6 Build Health, 5.8 Visual Usability, and 6.2 Intent Alignment points. GameCoder-27B reaches 63.9/57.0/54.1 and outperforms every direct LLM baseline on Build Health and Intent Alignment.
custo_vs_baseline: No numeric cost comparison is reported; ablation shows bounded iterative debugging improves reliability and begins to plateau after three iterations.
limitacoes: Even the full OpenGame leaves about 34.9% of weighted mechanical requirements partially or fully unsatisfied; strategy and Puzzle/UI genres remain difficult because silent logic desynchronization may not trigger compiler or runtime errors.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on OpenGame-Bench prompt design, VLM judging, Phaser instruction constraints for baselines, valid-run filtering, browser execution, seed averaging, and whether web-game tasks generalize to other software domains or engines.
